courses.name: Master of Business Administration
courses.summary: Compare MBA programmes by official format, duration, work-experience requirement, curriculum, tuition fees and location. Universities may offer different study routes for different applicant profiles.
courses.metaDescription: Compare MBA programmes abroad by university, duration, work experience, tuition fees, curriculum, scholarships and admission requirements.

universities.name: RMIT University Vietnam
universities.city: Ho Chi Minh City and Hanoi

cover.rights: Official RMIT University website asset used to identify the institution
cover.altText: RMIT University Vietnam Saigon South campus at night

universities.summary: RMIT University Vietnam is the Asian campus of Melbourne-based RMIT University. It offers Australian qualifications in English across business, technology, communication and design at campuses in Ho Chi Minh City and Hanoi.
universities.campusLifestyle: RMIT Vietnam operates the Saigon South campus in Ho Chi Minh City and a city campus in Hanoi. Students use teaching spaces, libraries, technology facilities, clubs, wellbeing services and careers support in an international English-speaking environment.
universities.cityProfile: Ho Chi Minh City is Vietnam’s largest commercial centre, while Hanoi is the national capital. Programme availability differs by campus, so applicants must check the location shown on the official programme page before applying.
universities.practicalExposure: The university uses projects, case work and industry-connected learning. MBA teaching includes applied management work, leadership, strategy, analytics and business problem-solving. Programme-specific practical components must be confirmed from the current handbook.
universities.hostelOverview: RMIT Vietnam does not present one compulsory university residence model for all students. International students receive pre-arrival and accommodation guidance and should confirm housing independently for their selected campus and intake.
universities.dietarySupport: Both campus cities provide broad local and international food options. Students with allergies, medical diets or religious requirements should verify suitable arrangements near their campus and accommodation.
universities.safetyOverview: International students must maintain the correct RMIT-sponsored student visa and follow Vietnamese immigration requirements. Campus support is available, but students remain responsible for personal safety, insurance and compliance.
universities.studentSupport: International support covers application guidance, visa invitation documentation, pre-arrival communication, airport pickup guidance, orientation and general student services. Academic, wellbeing and careers services are also available.
whyChoose: English-medium Australian qualifications delivered in Vietnam
Dedicated full-degree application and visa process for international students
Business and technology programmes with industry-connected learning
Campuses in two major Vietnamese cities
thingsToConsider: Tuition is charged in Vietnamese dong and can change by intake
Programme and campus availability must be checked individually
International students require an RMIT-sponsored Vietnamese student visa
Housing and living costs are additional to tuition
bestFitFor: Students seeking an English-medium international university in Vietnam
Applicants comfortable studying in a large Asian city
MBA applicants comparing one-and-a-half and two-year study routes
Students who value an Australian university qualification in Southeast Asia
recognitionBadges: RMIT College of Business and Law — AACSB accredited member

faq.question: Can international students apply to RMIT Vietnam?
faq.answer: Yes. RMIT publishes a separate full-degree application process for non-Vietnamese passport holders and supports the visa invitation process after an offer is accepted.

faq.question: Where is RMIT University Vietnam located?
faq.answer: RMIT Vietnam operates campuses in Ho Chi Minh City and Hanoi. The available campus depends on the programme and intake.

faq.question: Is teaching conducted in English?
faq.answer: The listed MBA is taught in English. Applicants must meet the programme’s English-language requirement or an accepted pathway or equivalency.

faq.question: How many intakes are available?
faq.answer: RMIT Vietnam generally operates February or March, June or July, and October intakes, but individual programme availability and deadlines can differ.

faq.question: How is MBA tuition calculated?
faq.answer: RMIT charges by enrolled course in Vietnamese dong. The total depends on whether the student completes the 12-course advanced-standing route or the standard 16-course route.

faq.question: Does RMIT help with the student visa?
faq.answer: After offer acceptance and the required payment, RMIT prepares a visa invitation letter. The final visa decision remains with Vietnamese immigration authorities.

admissionsContent.overview: International students apply online after checking programme and country-specific entry requirements.

eligibility.intro: Admission depends on the selected programme and prior qualification.
items: Recognised bachelor’s degree for MBA entry

admissionSteps: Prepare and submit the online application
Accept the offer and pay the required deposit

eligibleAudiences: Applicants with a recognised bachelor’s degree
restrictions: Advanced standing depends on prior business study; work experience may be required in specified cases

programmes.feeNotes: The 2026 standard 16-course MBA fee is VND 895,872,000. Eligible applicants with advanced standing complete 12 courses for VND 671,904,000. Fees are indicative and charged by course.

teachingPhases.details: Students complete 16 courses, or 12 courses when approved for advanced standing, across management, leadership, strategy and applied business study.
